label: PI-019 / PI-050 / PI-053 / PI-068 / PI-080 implementation — second autonomous orchestrator run. Doug asked Claude Code to triage all 68 Open PIs and dispatch sub-agents for the ones completable without further input. Triage selected five low-risk, file-disjoint, no-design-decision PIs and dispatched them as five parallel sub-agents. PI-053 added the Commit model to exporter._EXPORT_TABLES (commits.json now regenerates). PI-068 authored specifications/README.md as the canonical manifest. PI-080 registered the conversation_orchestrates_conversation reference kind (vocab + Alembic migration 0022). PI-050 added --commits explicit-list mode to enumerate_commits.py. PI-019 fixed the YAML layout validator's category resolution to union across the deploy batch. All five resolve in this one close-out.

session.session_title: Second autonomous orchestrator run — triage 68 Open PIs, dispatch 5 parallel sub-agents resolving PI-019/050/053/068/080
session.session_description:
Single Claude Code session running the parallel-agent orchestrator pattern for the second time (first was SES-101 resolving PI-091/092/093). Doug's prompt: 'you are an orcastrator. please review all open PI's and spawn a subagent to complete the PI if it can be completed without input from me.'

---

Topics covered:

Seed prompt: "you are an orcastrator. please review all open PI's and spawn a subagent to complete the PI if it can be completed without input from me."

1. Pulled all 68 Open PIs from the live API. Categorized each by autonomy-readiness: (a) clear technical scope with no design decisions = autonomous-ready; (b) multi-option resolution or scope decision = deferred for user input; (c) AI-content backfill = autonomous-doable but reviewable so deferred; (d) shelved per CLAUDE.md upstream blockers (PI-045, PI-049). Selected five for parallel dispatch: PI-019, PI-050, PI-053, PI-068, PI-080.

2. Verified preconditions before dispatching: PI-076's orchestrator-planning.md exists; PI-053's Commit model exists at access/models.py:1485; specifications/ contains only three files (master-crmbuilder-PRD.md, glossary.md, governance-recording-rules.md) so PI-068's manifest scope is small; current alembic head is 0021 so PI-080's migration becomes 0022; PI-019's _validate_layout exists at config_loader.py:738 with field_categories at line 783 (drifted from PI body's stale line 663 reference, agent told to grep). PI-018 ruled out for this batch because it touches the same config_loader.py as PI-019 — sequencing risk.

3. Dispatched five parallel sub-agents in a single tool-call message, each with a file-disjoint scope and explicit do-not-touch instructions. Each brief covered: PI body summary, exact files to touch, test requirements, lint command, commit message format, what NOT to touch.

4. Sub-agent reports came back over the next ~7 minutes. All five committed cleanly. Commit graph interleaved with a parallel SES-102 session that landed PI-096 (executive_summary backfill on 93 PIs) between SES-101 close-out and my first sub-agent commit — no file conflicts, the commits stacked linearly on main.

5. PI-068 — author specifications/README.md manifest (commit cac3909). Found the L1/L2/L3 vocabulary only informally referenced in master-crmbuilder-PRD.md §1; documented the in-use distinction (L1/L2 = product-name-neutral, L3 = internal) while explicitly flagging the lack of a formal standalone definition. 74-line manifest with seven sections.

6. PI-053 — add Commit model to exporter._EXPORT_TABLES (commit bd55db0). One-line list insertion plus model import. Test confirms commits.json regenerates; row count matches API (31 records).

7. PI-019 — cross-file category resolution (commit 07fa7b2). Added a new categories_by_entity dict + field_categories_for(entity_name) accessor to ProgramContext (mirroring the existing fields_by_entity + field_names_for pair), then changed _validate_layout to union the local entity's categories with the batch context's. Four new tests including legacy/cross-file/error/accessor coverage.

8. PI-050 — extend enumerate_commits.py with --commits mode (commit 2903f77). 9 new tests; manual two-SHA invocation confirmed correct chronological ordering. Agent noted the PI body referenced --since-sha but the actual script uses --engagement-db-export — adapted the mutual-exclusion warning to the real flag name.

9. PI-080 — register conversation_orchestrates_conversation (commit 5333f46). Three coordinated changes per CLAUDE.md rule: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS, _kinds_for_pair, Alembic migration 0022 extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK. Migration ran cleanly against CRMBUILDER.db. Agent skipped live-API POST verification because the running daemon still has pre-PI-080 vocab in memory and a restart would have disrupted the four sibling agents; equivalent coverage via isolated-test-DB API test (passed).

10. Net result: five PIs resolved in roughly seven wall-clock minutes of parallel sub-agent work, plus orchestrator triage + close-out time. The orchestrator pattern continues to prove out for clear-scope file-disjoint PI batches.

---

In flight at end:

- 60 Open PIs remain deferred for user input (design decisions, scope questions, stakeholder review needs, backfill content authoring, large architectural work). PI-046/077/016/007/070 specifically have multiple-option resolutions worth surfacing. PI-018 (oneToOne YAML schema) is autonomy-ready but was held out of this batch only because it touches the same config_loader.py as PI-019; could run as a singleton next.
- PI-045 / PI-049 remain shelved on Anthropic upstream OAuth bug per CLAUDE.md.
- PI-083 (area-field backfill) waits on PI-076 (area-field schema) which itself sits in deferred category.
- The running crmbuilder-v2-api daemon still has pre-PI-080 vocab in Python memory. The new conversation_orchestrates_conversation kind is present in the DB CHECK constraint (migration 0022 applied) but won't be writable via the API until the daemon restarts. This close-out doesn't write that kind, so the gap is benign for this apply.
session.session_executive_summary: Second autonomous run of the parallel-orchestrator pattern. Doug asked Claude Code to triage all 68 Open PIs and dispatch sub-agents for the ones completable without further input. Triage selected five low-risk file-disjoint no-design-decision PIs — PI-019 (YAML layout validator cross-file fix), PI-050 (enumerate_commits.py --commits flag), PI-053 (Commit model into exporter), PI-068 (specifications/README.md manifest), PI-080 (conversation_orchestrates_conversation reference kind). All five resolved cleanly in roughly seven minutes of parallel sub-agent work plus orchestrator triage and close-out time. 60 Open PIs remain deferred pending user input on design decisions, scope choices, or backfill content review.

conversation.conversation_title: Second autonomous orchestrator run — triage 68 Open PIs, dispatch 5 parallel sub-agents
conversation.conversation_purpose: Continue exercising the parallel-orchestrator pattern by triaging all Open planning items in the CRMBUILDER engagement and autonomously executing any PI whose technical scope is clear enough to complete without further user input. Surface the deferred set so the user can make decisions on the multi-option PIs in a later session. Validate the pattern's repeatability beyond SES-101's first run.
conversation.conversation_description: Single conversation under SES-103 covering the orchestrator triage, five-sub-agent parallel dispatch against disjoint files, sub-agent result aggregation, and close-out authoring. The work followed the SES-101 pattern: triage → file-disjointness analysis → parallel dispatch → wait for completions → assemble bundled close-out. Each sub-agent owned its own implementation + tests + lint + commit and reported back with SHA + diff summary + decisions made under ambiguity. Five resolutions land in this one close-out: PI-019 (YAML layout validator cross-file category), PI-050 (enumerate_commits.py --commits flag), PI-053 (Commit model into exporter), PI-068 (specifications/README.md manifest), PI-080 (conversation_orchestrates_conversation vocab + migration 0022).

commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-068 — author specifications/README.md manifest

Creates the entry-point manifest for the specifications/ directory.
Names the current canonical documents (master-crmbuilder-PRD.md,
governance-recording-rules.md, glossary.md), recommended reading
order, and cross-references the supersession ledger in CLAUDE.md.

Goal per PI-068: 'where is the definitive document for X?' is
answerable by reading specifications/README.md first.

From PI-068.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-053 — add Commit model to exporter._EXPORT_TABLES so commits.json regenerates
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-053 — add Commit model to exporter._EXPORT_TABLES so commits.json regenerates

The Commit model (v0.7+ code-change governance entity) was missing from
the _EXPORT_TABLES list in access/exporter.py, so the snapshot regenerator
never wrote a commits.json file. Every other governance entity (workstream,
conversation, reference_book*, work_ticket, close_out_payload, deposit_event)
is present in the list. Adding Commit closes the gap so commits.json
now regenerates alongside the other governance snapshots.

Discovered during SES-078 (PI-002) close-out apply when CM-0005 was
created in the DB but no commits.json file appeared on disk. From PI-053.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-019 — cross-file category resolution in YAML layout validator
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-019 — cross-file category resolution in YAML layout validator

Fixes _validate_layout in espo_impl/core/config_loader.py so the
field_categories set unions across all YAML files in the deploy batch
(via ProgramContext) and the current entity's own fields, mirroring
the existing pattern for field names a few lines above. Previously
the category check was file-scoped, so a layout panel referencing
category="Sponsor Profile" only validated if the SAME YAML declared
a field with that category — forcing operators to over-bundle
definitions in one file.

Surfaced on the CBM MN-Account / MN-Partner / MN-Sponsor file-split
case. PI-019.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-050 — extend enumerate_commits.py with --commits explicit-list mode
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-050 — extend enumerate_commits.py with --commits explicit-list mode

Adds a --commits SHA1,SHA2,... flag that emits commit-record JSON for
exactly the named SHAs in chronological order, bypassing the
since..HEAD range entirely. Needed when multiple workstreams interleave
commits on main and the since..HEAD range would pull in unrelated
commits.

The since..HEAD assumption (single active workstream per repo) breaks
when parallel workstreams land between a workstream's parent commit
and its closure HEAD. SES-074 (PI-030's first end-to-end use)
discovered this — 8 commits NOT owned by PI-030 sat within the natural
range. SES-074 worked around it by hand-curating SHAs into the
payload's commits section per DEC-233; --commits formalizes that
pattern so it scales.

From PI-050.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-080 — register conversation_orchestrates_conversation reference kind
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-080 — register conversation_orchestrates_conversation reference kind

Adds the relationship kind connecting an orchestrator conversation to
each of its child agents' conversations. Without it, the governance
timeline can't express the parent-child structure of a parallel run
and audit queries can't walk from an orchestrator session to its
children.

Three coordinated changes per the CLAUDE.md rule for adding relationship
kinds:

- REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS grows the new kind.
- _kinds_for_pair admits (conversation, conversation) →
  conversation_orchestrates_conversation alongside the existing
  conversation_follows_from / conversation_relates_to edges.
- Alembic migration 0022 extends refs.relationship_kind CHECK to
  include the new kind. Runs through the post-PI-074
  _build_migration_engine() with foreign_keys=OFF automatically.

The desktop UI's references-create dialog picks the new option up
automatically through RELATIONSHIP_RULES; no UI code change required.

From PI-080.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
